question: I am getting the error "your connection is not private" when trying to log in to the system. What should I do?
answer: To fix this problem, clear your browser's "cache" and "cookies". Update your browser to the latest version. If the error persists, try using a different browser.

question: The system gives the error "Your access to work with the database is blocked." Why and what is the solution?
answer: The error "Your access to the database is blocked" is due to one or more of the following reasons: 1- The system address is incorrect. The correct address was sent in the SMS containing the password. 2- You have selected the wrong login panel (users or engineers or citizens). Use the engineers panel only if you are a supervising engineer. 3- You do not have access to the system. In this case, you need to send an official email to the provincial SIMAC support office.

question: The system gives the error "The username and/or password or one-time code is not valid, please try again." Why?
answer: It is displayed due to incorrect user ID or password entry. In this case, you need to make sure the Caps Lock button is on, the keyboard language is set to EN, and the user ID (national number) is correct and try again. If the problem persists, get a one-time password again.

question: When logging into the system, it gives the error "Please contact the system administrator." Why?
answer: This error is due to entering more than the allowed password incorrectly. You need to submit an official form to the provincial support office to reactivate access.

question: As a counter office/technical and engineering office/surveying office/surveying engineer, what should I do to cooperate with SIMAC?
answer: To request cooperation with the SIMAC project, please contact the SIMAC office in your province.

question: What should I do to obtain access to register/create a file in SIMAC?
answer: To receive access, register the field/form a file in SIMAC and send it to the support email along with the necessary documents.

question: How do I change my mobile number in the system?
answer: Go to the home page of the system and verify your identity details with your new mobile number through the Heda authentication system link.

question: What is my username and password to log in to the system?
answer: When you first log in to the system, click on the "Get Password" link. On the page that opens, enter your national ID and mobile phone number registered in the Heda system in the User ID and Mobile Phone fields, respectively.

question: The system gives the error "Number of sessions exceeded the allowed limit". Why?
answer: The system displays the error "Number of sessions exceeded the allowed limit" because your account is open on other devices and browsers. To resolve the issue, you need to log out of your account on other devices and browsers. If the issue persists, contact the provincial SIMAC support office.

question: The system gives the error "Your identity has not been verified, please authenticate yourself from the Heda system section." Why?
answer: To fix the error "Your identity has not been verified, please authenticate from the Heda system section", authenticate using the "Heda Authentication System" link on the login page.

question: I am a SIMAK user. How do I authenticate in the Heda system?
answer: From the Useful Links menu on the home page, select the Heda Authentication System submenu and enter the Heda Authentication link.

question: I have forgotten my password. How can I get a new password and change my password?
answer: To receive a new password, click on the "I forgot my password" link on the first page of the system login and enter your national ID and mobile phone number, and a temporary password will be sent to you by SMS. After logging in with the temporary password, you can change your password. Please note that if you are a citizen, log in to the system from the citizens panel, if you are a supervising engineer, from the engineers panel, and otherwise from the users panel.

question: I am getting the error "User ID and mobile phone number do not match" when I receive my password, even though I entered my phone number and national ID correctly. What is the reason?
answer: The message "User ID and mobile phone number do not match" could be because the mobile phone number entered when receiving the password is different from the number registered in the system, in which case the information needs to be edited. To edit your personal information, you can click on the "Huda Authentication System" link on the home page of the SIMAC system from the Useful Links section and update your information in this system by entering your identity information and correct mobile phone number. Note that the mobile phone number must match the national number and be in the person's name. After authenticating in the Huda system, you can again receive your temporary password via SMS from the "Get Password" link.

question: I encountered the message "An unknown error has occurred" when logging into the system. What is the cause?
answer: To investigate the error that occurred, you need to send the information to the email address of the provincial support office.

question: Why is the one-time password not being sent to me?
answer: The limit for sending one-time passwords is 3 times per day. It should be noted that in some cases, due to network disruptions, sending SMS messages may be delayed. In this case, you need to request one-time passwords at appropriate intervals.

question: Why isn't the test link being sent to me?
answer: The test link will not be sent for the following reasons: 1- The user's email is incorrect or duplicated. 2- The user has received the test link twice this year.

question: What site should I log into to log into the SIMAC system? Should I use the simac.ir website address?
answer: To log in to your province's SIMAC system, first go to on.simactech.ca in this link https://on.simactech.ca

question: While working with the system, I encountered the error message "It is not possible to perform database-related operations." and I am unable to continue working with the system.
answer: You need to inform the provincial SIMAC support office about this.

question: I am a service bureau/surveyor user. I have recharged my account but the credit increase has not yet been applied to the system. What should I do?
answer: Go to the shopping cart section and click on the Modify shopping cart option to complete the purchase process. If you do not see the Modify shopping cart option, it means the purchase process is complete and in this case the amount will be returned to your bank account within 72 hours.

question: I encountered an "unknown error" message while working with the system. Please help.
answer: To fix the unknown error, refresh the page. If the error is not resolved, send your account details along with a screenshot of the error to the support email.

question: When will the SIMAC access exam be held?
answer: After completing and submitting the access request form, if you meet the requirements to participate in the exam, you will be notified of the exam date via email.

question: Can we log in to the site to register construction reports using a mobile phone?
answer: No, because the token is under Windows, you must log in to the system with a personal computer or laptop.

question: Due to the number of times I have entered the wrong password in the system, my access has been blocked. What is the solution?
answer: Inform your provincial support office about this so that your access can be reactivated.

question: Is it possible to activate the token in person?/ How do I activate the hardware token?
answer: You can activate the token yourself like this: Please connect the token to the system. Double-click on the token icon that is displayed at the bottom of the system. A window will be displayed for you, then click on login and enter your token password (if you have not changed the password, it is 1234). After entering the password, press the ok button, then enter the token information. From the left side of the image, click once on your first and last name written in English, then on the right side of the image, the word export or (certificate extraction) will be activated for you, click on it and in the opened window, write your name in English in the name field and save the file in the system. And send the relevant file,

question: I am getting the error "Username not found" when logging in. What is the cause?
answer: You must download the default file from the main SIMAC website and then install it on your system.

question: When charging my SIMAC account, the amount was deducted from my bank account and the transaction was successful, but my SIMAC account has not been charged yet. What should I do?
answer: Go to the shopping cart section and click on the Modify shopping cart option to complete the purchase process. If you do not see the Modify shopping cart option, it means the purchase process is complete and in this case the amount will be returned to your bank account within 72 hours.

question: How can I access SIMAC training files and videos?
answer: To access SIMAC educational files and videos, visit the lms educational system at https://lms.simacnashr.ir. Username, contact number, and password are your national number. In the My Lessons section, educational videos can be viewed and you should watch the videos in order from the first lesson. If your access to the educational system is not active, you need to send your request to the email address of the provincial support office.

question: I am a surveyor. I have watched the training videos related to the system and notified the office. What should I do?
answer: Your name will be sent to the central office for registration in the surveyors exam (the exam time is the first day of the even-numbered months of the year).

question: When I try to get my password, I get the message "An unknown error has occurred. Contact your system administrator" and I can't get my password. What is the reason?
answer: To investigate the problem, you need to send the information to the email address of the provincial support office.

question: The system gives a 504 error. What should I do?
answer: If you are confident in your internet connection, change your browser and try again.

question: The system gives a 404 error. What should I do?
answer: The problem will be resolved in a few minutes, please be patient.

question: Can I change my password in the system after receiving a temporary password?
answer: Yes. After receiving the temporary password, first log in to your main page of Kartab, then click the Change Password option at the bottom of the page to change your initial password.

question: I was sent an access SMS but the password has expired. What is the solution?
answer: You need to click Get Password again to have a new password sent to you.

question: Why is login slow?
answer: The problem may be with your internet. Please make sure your internet is stable. Or it may be due to a disruption in the infrastructure, which is temporary and will be resolved soon.
